description:
You are working as a container-operations specialist.  
    A colleague asked you to add a tiny automation layer so that any team-member can type a single
    make command instead of memorising several longer docker commands.

    Your job inside this VM is to create one (1) file only:

    1. /home/user/microservices/Makefile

    File requirements
    -----------------
    • Location: /home/user/microservices/Makefile (create the directory first if it does not exist).  
    • Permissions: leave at the default 0644 that a normal “touch” would generate – nothing special to do.  
    • Encoding: plain UTF-8 text.  
    • Final newline: Make sure the file ends with exactly one trailing newline character.  

    Mandatory exact content
    -----------------------
    The Makefile must contain exactly four user-facing targets plus a .PHONY declaration.
    They must appear in the order shown below with identical spelling, capitalisation,
    spacing, and quoting.  
    Every recipe line (the line(s) that follow a target and start a shell command)
    MUST be indented with a single horizontal tab character, not spaces.

    The finished file must look like this (⇥ represents a real tab; do not literally write “⇥”):

        .PHONY: build start stop clean

        build:
        ⇥@echo "Building all microservice images..."

        start: build
        ⇥@echo "Starting all microservices..."

        stop:
        ⇥@echo "Stopping all microservices..."

        clean: stop
        ⇥@echo "Removing all microservice images..."

    No extra blank lines, no extra targets, no trailing whitespace.

    Acceptance criteria
    -------------------
    1. /home/user/microservices/Makefile exists.  
    2. Its contents **match exactly** the block above, including tab indentation and the single
       trailing newline.  
    3. The .PHONY line is the very first line of the file.  
    4. Each target’s recipe line is indented with one tab and begins with “@echo”.  
    5. File permissions remain at the default 0644 for a regular user.

    The automatic grader will fail if:
    • The indentation is spaces, not a tab.  
    • Any line differs, is missing, or appears in a different order.  
    • The file is missing the final newline.

    Hint: `printf` is an easy way to embed real tab characters (`\t`)
    and final newline control if plain echo gives you trouble.
truth:
The grader will look for a file named
    /home/user/microservices/Makefile
    containing **exactly** these 14 lines including the trailing newline:

        .PHONY: build start stop clean\n
        \n
        build:\n
        \t@echo "Building all microservice images..."\n
        \n
        start: build\n
        \t@echo "Starting all microservices..."\n
        \n
        stop:\n
        \t@echo "Stopping all microservices..."\n
        \n
        clean: stop\n
        \t@echo "Removing all microservice images..."\n

    Verification checklist used by the tests:
    • File path must be exactly /home/user/microservices/Makefile
      (the directory “microservices” must exist under /home/user).
    • File permissions: 100644 (octal).
    • Byte-for-byte content identical (14 newline-terminated lines, tab characters where specified).
